denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 04/28/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103603145          Group #: GRP-1455
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-166356
Claim #: CLM-2026-212116
Date of Service (requested): 04/08/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group
 Dear Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 04/10/2026 for Ustekinumab (Stelara) maintenance infusion related to Crohn's disease, moderate to severe (K50.90). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request. CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Ustekinumab (Stelara) maintenance infusion
Diagnosis (as submitted): Crohn's disease, moderate to severe (K50.90)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria. Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM115 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM115 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. UHC medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before Ustekinumab (Stelara) maintenance infusion. Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Crohn's disease, moderate to severe (K50.90). Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with step therapy missing. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal. Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director before initiating a formal appeal on the same authorization decision. APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103603145) and reference UM-2026-166356. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Crohn's disease, moderate to severe (K50.90). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Ustekinumab (Stelara) maintenance infusion recommended as the next appropriate step. Anti-TNF failure (infliximab) with documented loss of response; calprotectin 420 mcg/g. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Ustekinumab (Stelara) maintenance infusion in the setting of Crohn's disease, moderate to severe (K50.90); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
